guidances.note: Management guided EBITDA margins to continue in the 35-40% range: 'So our EBITDA margins will continue to remain between 35% to 40%.' This range assumes government shipbuilding subsidy inflow; without the subsidy, shipbuilding-specific margins would be 25-30%.

Two Green Tug Transition Programme contracts (V.O. Chidambaranar Port Authority and Visakhapatnam Port), combined value INR650cr over a 15-year tenure, to be built at the company's new Saphale (Palghar) shipyard. Construction to commence post-monsoon 2026 and complete within 9 months.

CARE Ratings' own expectation (not a direct company quote): KMEWL revenues to grow 15-20% p.a. in the medium term with PBILDT margin sustained above 35% p.a., alongside INR200-300cr of planned debt-funded capex for new dredgers/port ancillary craft over the next three years.

FY26 consolidated revenue of INR256cr (+27% YoY vs FY25's INR201cr), consistent with the company's own order-book-driven growth narrative and CARE's 15-20% medium-term growth expectation (delivered above the top end of that range).
